heroHeadline: Somalia: SNA Kills Two Al-Shabaab Commanders in Lower Shabelle; 500,000+ Displaced by Drought in 2026; 18 Days to Presidential Term Expiry
heroSubtitle: Somali National Army units from the 26th, 6th, and 60th divisions conducted a targeted operation in Madulow, Lower Shabelle on April 27, killing Al-Shabaab commanders Hassan Biid and Abdullahi Garweyne and dismantling a militant base — the latest HVT strike in the ongoing Operation Bader campaign. The operation comes as Somalia's humanitarian crisis worsens sharply: Al Jazeera's April 27 gallery documents more than 500,000 people displaced since January 2026 (90% by drought), with 6.5 million Somalis facing severe food insecurity, 1.85 million children under five at acute malnutrition risk, and only 13.4% of the $852 million Humanitarian Response Plan funded — while WFP has cut nutrition support from 400,000 to 90,000 pregnant and breastfeeding women and 200+ health facilities have closed. On the maritime front, the Palau-flagged fuel tanker Honour 25 with 17 multinational crew remains under pirate control off the Puntland coast entering Day 3 with no ransom demand issued and negotiations expected to take weeks. The constitutional crisis enters its penultimate phase with 18 days until President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud's May 15 term expiry: the Somali Future Council's Salvation Summit ultimatum remains active, opposition threatens a parallel election within one month if talks are refused, and Al-Shabaab's eight-day hold on Adan Yabal in Hiiraan Region continues unchallenged as the SNA/Macawisley counter-offensive stalls. Israel's cabinet approval of its first ambassador to Somaliland on April 26 — deepening the FGS sovereignty crisis — still draws sharp condemnation from Mogadishu with no diplomatic resolution in sight.
footerNote: Data sourced from ACLED, OCHA, UNSOM, AFRICOM, EU EPF, EU NAVFOR, Combined Maritime Forces, UN Monitoring Group, and major international outlets. Casualty figures contested where noted.
